steps.content: Apply the rule $x^{a}\cdot x^{b}=x^{a+b}$ to each product. First term: $x^{-5+2}y^{4}z^{3+6}=x^{-3}y^{4}z^{9}$. Second term: $x^{-5}y^{4+7}z^{3+(-5)}=x^{-5}y^{11}z^{-2}$.

Combine to get $x^{-3}y^{4}z^{9}+x^{-5}y^{11}z^{-2}$. Evaluate each choice:
  • "${x}^{-3}{z}^{9}+{y}^{11}{z}^{-2}$": missing the $y^{4}$ factor in the first term and the $x^{-5}$ factor in the second term. Incorrect.
  • "${x}^{-3}{z}^{9}+{x}^{-5}{z}^{-2}$": missing $y^{4}$ in the first term and $y^{11}$ in the second term. Incorrect.
  • "${x}^{-3}{y}^{4}{z}^{9}+{y}^{7}{z}^{-5}$": the first term is correct but the second term retains the original $y^{7}z^{-5}$ without the $x^{-5}y^{4}$ factors applied. Incorrect.
  • "${x}^{-3}{y}^{4}{z}^{9}+{x}^{-5}{y}^{11}{z}^{-2}$": matches both computed terms exactly. That matches the correct answer - D is correct.
